reference: GATE CS — Linear Algebra / Matrices and Systems

map.condition: Need to know how many solutions exist
System is homogeneous (Ax = 0), find non-trivial solution
Count free variables / dimension of solution space
Check if non-homogeneous system has unique solution
Find solution using inverse (square, non-singular A)

formulas.name: Rouché–Capelli Theorem (Consistency Condition)
formulas.plain: Ax = b is consistent iff rank(A) = rank([A|b])
formulas.whenToUse: To determine whether a non-homogeneous system has at least one solution
formulas.trap: rank(A) = rank([A|b]) does NOT mean unique solution; uniqueness also requires rank = n
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Condition for Unique Solution
formulas.plain: rank(A) = rank([A|b]) = n => unique solution
formulas.whenToUse: When system is consistent and rank equals number of unknowns
formulas.trap: Applies only to non-homogeneous; for square A this means det(A) ≠ 0
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Condition for Infinitely Many Solutions
formulas.plain: rank(A) = rank([A|b]) = r < n => infinitely many solutions
formulas.whenToUse: When system is consistent but rank is less than number of unknowns

terms.means: common rank of A and augmented matrix

formulas.trap: Number of free variables is n - r, not n - m
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Condition for No Solution (Inconsistent)
formulas.plain: rank(A) < rank([A|b]) => no solution (inconsistent)
formulas.whenToUse: When the augmented matrix has higher rank than the coefficient matrix

terms.means: rank of augmented matrix; exceeds rank(A) by exactly 1 when inconsistent

formulas.trap: rank([A|b]) can exceed rank(A) by at most 1; a gap of 2 is impossible
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Trivial Solution Always Exists
formulas.plain: Ax = 0 always has the trivial solution x = 0
formulas.whenToUse: Any homogeneous system is always consistent (rank(A) = rank([A|0]))
formulas.trap: The question asks for non-trivial solutions, which require rank(A) < n
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Condition for Non-Trivial Solution
formulas.latex: Ax = 0 \text{ has non-trivial solution} \iff \text{rank}(A) < n \iff \det(A) = 0 \text{ (if square)}
formulas.plain: Ax = 0 has non-trivial solution iff rank(A) < n, equivalently det(A) = 0 for square A
formulas.whenToUse: When asked whether a homogeneous system has solutions other than x = 0
formulas.trap: For non-square (m < n) systems, non-trivial solutions always exist since rank(A) ≤ m < n
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Dimension of Null Space (Nullity)
formulas.plain: nullity(A) = n - rank(A)
formulas.whenToUse: To find how many free variables / dimension of solution space of Ax = 0

terms.means: set of all x such that Ax = 0 (null space / kernel)

formulas.trap: Nullity counts free variables, not total variables; it equals zero when the only solution is trivial
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Rank-Nullity Theorem
formulas.plain: rank(A) + nullity(A) = n (number of columns)
formulas.whenToUse: Relates rank and dimension of null space for any m x n matrix
formulas.trap: n is the number of COLUMNS, not rows; for m x n matrices rank ≤ min(m,n)
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Rank Bound
formulas.plain: rank(A) <= min(m, n)
formulas.whenToUse: To bound maximum possible rank and minimum possible nullity
formulas.trap: Under-determined system (m < n) always has rank ≤ m < n, guaranteeing infinitely many solutions if consistent
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Rank of Product
formulas.plain: rank(AB) <= min(rank(A), rank(B))
formulas.whenToUse: When rank of a matrix product must be bounded

terms.means: matrix product (A is m x k, B is k x n)

formulas.trap: Equality does not always hold; rank can drop through multiplication
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Solution via Inverse (Square Non-Singular)
formulas.plain: If det(A) != 0 then x = A^{-1} b (unique solution)
formulas.whenToUse: When A is square and non-singular; gives closed-form unique solution

terms.means: inverse of A; exists iff det(A) ≠ 0

formulas.trap: If det(A) = 0, inverse does not exist; system may have 0 or ∞ solutions
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Cramer's Rule (for small square systems)
formulas.plain: x_i = det(A_i) / det(A), where A_i is A with column i replaced by b
formulas.whenToUse: Quick evaluation of individual unknowns in 2x2 or 3x3 non-singular systems

terms.means: matrix A with i-th column replaced by b

formulas.trap: Cramer's rule is only valid when det(A) ≠ 0; computationally expensive for large n
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Singular Matrix Cases
formulas.plain: det(A) = 0 => 0 solutions (inconsistent) or inf solutions (consistent but rank < n)
formulas.whenToUse: When the determinant is zero, use rank comparison to decide between no solution and infinite solutions

terms.means: A is singular; no unique solution

formulas.trap: det(A) = 0 does NOT automatically mean infinite solutions — system could be inconsistent
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Row Echelon Form (REF) — Pivot Count = Rank
formulas.latex: \text{rank}(A) = \text{number of non-zero rows in REF of } A
formulas.plain: rank(A) = number of non-zero rows after row reduction to REF
formulas.whenToUse: To compute rank by row-reducing the coefficient or augmented matrix

terms.means: Row Echelon Form obtained by Gaussian elimination

formulas.trap: Row operations preserve rank but scale/swap rows do not change rank; adding a multiple of one row to another does not change det
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Back Substitution (after REF)
formulas.latex: \text{Free variables} = n - r; \quad \text{assign parameters } t_1, t_2, \ldots, t_{n-r}
formulas.plain: Free variables = n - r; express pivot variables in terms of n - r free parameters
formulas.whenToUse: After reducing to REF/RREF to write the general solution
formulas.trap: Pivot columns correspond to pivot variables; non-pivot columns correspond to free variables — do not confuse the two
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: General Solution of Non-Homogeneous System
formulas.plain: x = x_particular + x_homogeneous, where x_h satisfies Ax_h = 0
formulas.whenToUse: Decompose any solution into a particular solution plus the null space

terms.means: any particular solution satisfying Ax = b
general solution of Ax = 0 (null space vector)

formulas.trap: x_p is not unique; any solution can serve as particular; x_h spans an (n-r)-dimensional subspace
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Dimension of Solution Space
formulas.plain: dim(solution space) = n - rank(A) when system is consistent
formulas.whenToUse: Counting degrees of freedom (free parameters) in the solution set

terms.means: solution set (affine subspace when b ≠ 0, vector subspace when b = 0)

formulas.trap: For Ax = b with b ≠ 0, S is an affine subspace (coset), not a subspace through origin
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Row Independence & Unique Solution Equivalence
formulas.latex: \text{Rows of } A \text{ are linearly independent} \iff \text{rank}(A) = m \iff \text{row space} = \mathbb{R}^m
formulas.plain: Rows are LI iff rank(A) = m (full row rank) — system consistent for every b
formulas.whenToUse: When asked if the system is consistent for ALL right-hand sides b
formulas.trap: Full row rank (rank = m) guarantees consistency for all b but NOT uniqueness if m < n
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Over-Determined System (m > n)
formulas.latex: m > n \Rightarrow \text{generally inconsistent; consistent only if } b \in \text{col}(A)
formulas.plain: More equations than unknowns => likely inconsistent unless b is in column space of A
formulas.whenToUse: When number of equations exceeds number of unknowns

terms.means: column space of A; set of all Ax for all x

formulas.trap: Even in over-determined systems, if the extra equations are linear combinations of others, consistent solutions exist
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Under-Determined System (m < n)
formulas.latex: m < n \Rightarrow \text{if consistent, infinitely many solutions (}n - r \geq n - m > 0\text{)}
formulas.plain: Fewer equations than unknowns => if consistent, always infinite solutions
formulas.whenToUse: When number of unknowns exceeds number of equations and system is consistent

terms.means: at least n - m free variables since rank ≤ m < n

formulas.trap: Under-determined system can still be inconsistent if the equations contradict each other
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations

formulas.name: Parameter (λ) for Existence of Non-Trivial or Specific Solutions
formulas.plain: Set det(A(lambda)) = 0 to find lambda values giving non-trivial/multiple solutions
formulas.whenToUse: GATE-style: given a system with parameter λ, find values for which solution type changes

terms.means: scalar parameter in entries of A or b

formulas.trap: After finding λ from det = 0, always substitute back to check if system is actually consistent (no solution vs infinite solutions)
formulas.reference: Linear Algebra / System of Linear Equations
